topic: North Pole cosmic mountain — verify EXP-216 integration (corrected six-text list + Zaphon-Zion kernel + accounting preemptive)
change_summary: EXP-216 integrated: detail_evidence fully replaced (2256→4647 chars) correcting the material strawman (six-text list now Isaiah 14:13, Psalm 48:2, Job 26:7, Job 37:22, Ezekiel 1:4, 1 Enoch 17–18 instead of the wrong Genesis/Hildegard list), adding a Zaphon-Zion kernel-of-truth paragraph, and appending the 'accounting is the point' preemptive at the end of argument (3). finding, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict also replaced. detail_verdict_text was NOT touched.
our_argument_summary: WIN-031 remains classified Unfalsifiable. The detail_evidence now opens by correctly naming the dome's six actual texts, concedes via a Zaphon-Zion paragraph that the convergence is real and scholarly-attested, then argues the claim fails on three independent axes: (1) retrodiction vs prediction, (2) non-discriminating axis-mundi archetype across six named cosmologies with mutually contradictory geometries, (3) the dome's own monitor.py excludes WIN-031 from automated testing but counts it in the 94.5% scorecard — a category error. tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, and finding carry the scorecard-accounting critique to surface text.

EXP-216_six_text_list_correction_ISS-135.verification: detail_evidence now names all six dome-site-actual texts exactly — Isaiah 14:13, Psalm 48:2, Job 26:7, Job 37:22, Ezekiel 1:4, 1 Enoch 17–18 — with a direct link to the dome WIN-031 entry. All five previously-wrong citations (Genesis 1:2, Job 37:18, Isaiah 40:22, Genesis 1:6–8, Hildegard's Scivias) are removed. grep of wins.json for the old citations within WIN-031 context returns empty. The strawman fix is clean.

EXP-216_zaphon_zion_kernel_of_truth.verification: Paragraph 2 of detail_evidence names the Zaphon-Zion typology, traces ṣāpôn ('north') as the shared Hebrew word in five of the six texts (Isaiah 14:13, Psalm 48:2, Job 26:7, Job 37:22, Ezekiel 1:4), locates Mount Zaphon (Jebel al-Aqraʿ, ~36°N, Syria-Turkey border) correctly relative to the Israelite kingdoms, and concedes 'The dome author is not inventing the convergence.' This is the house-style kernel-of-truth the c3 review flagged as missing. Factually accurate — Jebel al-Aqraʿ is at ~35.95°N on the Mediterranean coast, the Zaphon-Baal-to-Zion-Yahweh polemical reassignment is standard ANE cosmology scholarship (Clifford 1972, Levenson 1985).

EXP-216_accounting_preemptive_ISS-1362_I.verification: Argument (3) now explicitly names the charitable defense ('A charitable reading objects that this is circular: monitor.py is for empirical claims, and a textual-convergence WIN is non-empirical by classification, so of course it is excluded') and answers it ('The accounting is the point. We do not dispute the six-text convergence exists. We dispute that WIN-031 is counted toward the dome site's 69 confirmed / 94.5% accuracy scorecard…'). This closes the c3 advocate_mode defense_survives=3 best_defense at the detail level. Critique travels to tldr_verdict ('The dome still counts WIN-031 toward its 94.5% confirmation scorecard even though it is excluded from the author's own automated test harness') and finding ('WIN-031 is counted in the dome's 94.5% scorecard despite being excluded from its own automated test harness'). Three-surface propagation.

ISS-1359_sixth_text_unidentified.evidence: All six texts now named in detail_evidence, tldr_evidence. Resolved.

ISS-1362_argument_3_circular_framing.evidence: Argument (3) now explicitly names and answers the circular-defense charitable reading. Resolved.

ISS-1370_defense_EXP_needed.evidence: EXP-216 is the defense EXP and it has been integrated. advocate_mode.defense_survives should drop from 3 (c3) to 2 (c5) — one residual defense around flat-earth-cartography concession (see holes_found #2).

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (WIN-031 listed CONFIRMED, no monitor criteria)
raw-text/05-model.txt ('North pole mountain (WIN-031)' in architecture paragraph)
raw-text/04-coordinates.txt (North Pole: r=0, theta=-180.0 — geometric only)
data/sections.json part2b (monitor.py at commit 8e4bf9b with 39 domains)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-031 has no calculation pipeline. CONFIRMED is a static string in the dome site. The pole's geometric role (r=0 in H(r) = 8537 exp(-r/8619) km, axis mundi as toroidal source) is computed for other WINs (029, 038).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 100% hardcoded/label-only. No API call, no measurement, no comparison.
data_sources_used: None — WIN-031 uses no empirical data source
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: detail_evidence correctly sources the 39/28-domain figure; detail_verdict_text (which was NOT in EXP-216 scope) also correctly sources it from the ISS-1361 decider self-apply patch already integrated in c4. No discrepancies remain on the code-claim axis.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no domain dictionary keyed to WIN-031; sections.json part2b enumerates the 39 monitor.py domains and WIN-031 is not among them. The dome site's CONFIRMED label is a static string. Consistent with c4 tag.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: No standard-physics explanation of an axis mundi exists. Comparative-religion scholarship (Eliade 1959, Clifford 1972) describes the archetype as a cross-cultural literary motif but this is not 'standard physics' in any relabel-able sense.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Retrodiction (texts predate the model by roughly two to three millennia) rather than post-hoc-in-the-adopted-observation sense. No empirical measurement to adopt.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The theological label is not derived from dome geometry. The pole's geometric roles (r=0 origin, H(r) apex, aetheric source) are evaluated under separate WINs.

holes_found.description: Cross-surface inconsistency: detail_verdict_text (the verdict box that renders when the Verdict block is expanded) was not in EXP-216 scope and still does not carry the scorecard-accounting critique. detail_evidence, tldr_verdict, and finding now all say 'WIN-031 is counted in the 94.5% scorecard despite being excluded from the automated test harness' (or equivalent). detail_verdict_text still stops at 'the author's engineering implicitly agrees the theological claim is untestable' — which is half the argument. A reader who expands Verdict but not Evidence reads the untestability concession without reading the accounting critique that makes it matter. Four of four surfaces would be better than three of four for the load-bearing preemptive.
holes_found.recommendation: Append one sentence to detail_verdict_text after the monitor.py exclusion clause: e.g., 'Nevertheless, WIN-031 is counted in the dome's 69-confirmed / 94.5%-accuracy scorecard alongside empirically tested claims like Schumann frequency and SAA field strength — pricing theological convergence at par with refutable physical measurements is the category error.' Short targeted edit; do not replace the whole field. File as a follow-up issue (~ISS-1362b) since EXP-216 patch_instructions explicitly did not name detail_verdict_text.

holes_found.description: Residual advocate-mode defense not preempted: the detail_evidence paragraph on Zaphon argues that the ancient Israelites' 'north' reflects 'flat-earth cartographic direction' (real Mount Zaphon, ~36°N). A dome-defender can flip this as a kernel-of-truth extension: 'Precisely — ancient Israelites treated north as a cartographic direction toward a sacred mountain. That IS flat-earth cartography and it IS ancient witness to my model. You conceded the lexical-geographic pattern, then tried to define it out of existence by labelling it cartography, when cartography-of-a-flat-earth is exactly what the dome proposes.' The text does not preempt this. The critique of course works — 'north as cartographic direction' is equally true on a sphere (toward the pole) and on a dome disc — but it needs to be stated explicitly or the dome defender gets a free rhetorical win from the concession.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a short preemptive clause to argument (2), immediately after 'the texts' north reflects flat-earth cartographic direction, not the pole-apex of a dome disc.' Suggested: 'This cartographic convergence is equally compatible with a spherical earth, on which north points toward a geographic pole, or with any other axially-symmetric geometry — so the texts' directional usage distinguishes no cosmology in particular.' Keeps the paragraph tight while closing the residual defense. Open as ISS-1362c or fold into the ISS-1362b follow-up above.

holes_found.description: Characterisation of 1 Enoch 17-18 is slightly off. detail_evidence says '1 Enoch 17-18 then fills in a visionary cosmic geography with seven sacred mountains at the earth's center.' In 1 Enoch 18:6-9, Enoch's seven magnificent mountains are located beyond the waters 'to the west,' not at the earth's center. The central throne-mountain passage is more clearly in 1 Enoch 24-25. The broader point (1 Enoch contains a sacred-mountain cosmology compatible with the north-cosmic-mountain motif) survives, but a pedantic dome defender with a concordance could flag this as 'your own Bible scholarship is sloppy.' Not a load-bearing claim.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) soften to 'a visionary cosmic geography with sacred mountains' (drop the 'at the earth's center' specificity), or (b) move the specific claim to 1 Enoch 24-25 instead of 17-18. Option (a) is lower-touch and preserves the overall paragraph flow. Do not block on this; file as minor follow-up.

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence asserts all six texts are 'rooted in the Ancient Near East Mount Zaphon / Mount Zion tradition.' Strictly speaking, this holds for five of the six (the Hebrew Bible texts that use ṣāpôn or thematic north equivalents). 1 Enoch 17–18 is Second Temple apocalyptic pseudepigrapha with its own cosmological idiom; it inherits and extends ANE cosmological geography but is not 'rooted in' Zaphon-Zion in the same lexical-geographic sense. detail_evidence paragraph 2 handles this correctly ('Five of the six texts share the Hebrew word ṣāpôn…; 1 Enoch 17–18 then fills in…'). The TLDR elides that distinction. Nit-level imprecision, acceptable for a TLDR simplification but a pedantic defender could flag it.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: 'rooted in the Ancient Near East Mount Zaphon / Mount Zion tradition (with 1 Enoch 17–18 extending the motif in Second Temple apocalyptic)' — but this likely exceeds TLDR discipline. Lowest-priority. The detail_evidence handles the nuance; leave the TLDR as-is unless a reader specifically flags it.

stronger_arguments.description: The 'accounting is the point' framing would be even stronger if it quantified the scorecard impact. Specifically: WIN-031 contributes one to the numerator of the 69-confirmed figure AND one to the denominator of any future 'empirically tested' subset. If the dome author wishes to claim a confirmation rate against the empirical subset of WINs (i.e., those with monitor.py domains), WIN-031 is simultaneously counted-in and not-tested. The argument could name this more explicitly: 'This is the textbook category error — a claim counts in the scorecard if and only if it would also count in the monitor.py test harness. Claims that the author's own engineering classifies as untestable belong in a separate category (theological convergence, interpretive cross-reference) with a separate reporting line.' Don't block EXP-216 on this; log for a future polish pass.

Cross-WIN consistency: if we are going to strongly argue 'the dome's own engineering excludes this WIN from testing,' we should note that WIN-034 (copper dome / radio astronomy refutation, tagged Unfalsifiable) has the inverse problem — an empirical refutation that is classified as unfalsifiable. The Unfalsifiable verdict category is doing inconsistent work across WINs 031, 032, 034. A single cross-reference sentence in detail_extra could acknowledge this (e.g., 'See also SEC-4.6 on the Unfalsifiable category's heterogeneity'). This isn't a WIN-031 blocker; carry forward to next WIN-034 review.

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-216 is a substantial net improvement over c4. The material strawman (wrong six-text list) is cleanly corrected; the Zaphon-Zion kernel-of-truth paragraph is credible, accurately sourced (Jebel al-Aqraʿ at ~36°N is geographically correct; the Canaanite/Israelite polemical reassignment is standard scholarship), and shifts the tone from 'just-so dismissal' to 'scholarly engagement.' The accounting-preemptive closes the c3 defense_survives=3 best_defense at three of four surfaces. The factual floor under the critique is now defensible against a dome-defender with a concordance.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The verdict (Unfalsifiable) holds on the merits — retrodiction, non-discriminating archetype, and scorecard accounting are independent arguments and defeating all three is required. The residual holes (detail_verdict_text missing the scorecard line, flat-earth-cartography flipside unaddressed, 1 Enoch location imprecision, TLDR Zaphon-rooting overbreadth) are defensive polish rather than load-bearing critique. None of them change the verdict. All are specific, actionable, and small.

deeper_analysis:
EXP-216 is the largest patch WIN-031 has taken since Cycle 2. It fixed the most serious defect (wrong citation list — a strawman a defender could have used to invalidate the whole critique) and did it without leaking new factual errors, and it converted the tone from dismissive to scholarly. That is a substantial improvement. The EXP's own self-assessment ('defense_survives should drop to 1-2') is roughly correct — I score 2, with one residual being the flat-earth-cartography flipside of the Zaphon argument and one being the cross-surface gap in detail_verdict_text.

Considering the other side (per user preference for adversarial balance): a dome defender would probably concede the citation fix and the Zaphon-Zion kernel — these are not attackable. The remaining avenues are rhetorical: (1) the 'non-discriminating archetype' rebuttal is, technically, itself non-discriminating (no scriptural convergence could confirm any cosmology by this standard), but the EXP already pre-answers this in anticipated_objections. (2) The flat-earth-cartography concession in paragraph 4 is the genuine residual opening — the dome defender can say 'you have conceded the ancient texts treat north as a real cartographic direction, and my model is precisely a flat-earth cartography, so this part of your critique flatters my model.' This deserves a one-sentence preemptive. (3) The 1 Enoch location imprecision is a minor factual opening that could be fixed with a two-word edit. (4) detail_verdict_text surface-consistency is an editorial oversight from the EXP's scope, fixable with one appended sentence.

Quantitative verification: detail_evidence went from 2256 to 4647 chars (+2391, +106% — consistent with a full-field replacement adding a kernel paragraph + argument-3 expansion). tldr_evidence 298→589 (+98%), tldr_verdict 163→327 (+101%), finding 159→299 (+88%) — all consistent with EXP word_count_delta estimates (+20 to +17 words at each surface). detail_verdict_text length unchanged at 692, confirming it was not in EXP-216 scope. verdict label unchanged (Unfalsifiable), preserving cross-surface verdict consistency.

No prompt-injection material detected in the dome-site quoted fetch.

No new attack surface opened; four pre-existing polish opportunities remain. Verdict (Unfalsifiable) is correct and should not change.

citations_verified: Six dome texts in detail_evidence (Isaiah 14:13, Psalm 48:2, Job 26:7, Job 37:22, Ezekiel 1:4, 1 Enoch 17–18) match EXP-216 quoted_dome_text from https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html (fetched 2026-04-22T04:06 per EXP)

Hebrew word ṣāpôn ('north') and its presence in Isaiah 14:13 ('sides of the north'), Psalm 48:2 ('sides of the north'), Job 26:7 ('stretches out the north'), Job 37:22 ('out of the north'), Ezekiel 1:4 ('out of the north') — all verified against standard English translations (KJV/NRSV/ESV consistent on the directional language in each)
Mount Zaphon = Jebel al-Aqraʿ, ~36°N on Syria-Turkey border — verified (actual coordinates ~35.95°N 35.97°E, Mediterranean coast)
Zaphon-Zion polemical reassignment (Baal → Yahweh) — standard ANE cosmology scholarship, uncontroversial

Axis mundi cross-cultural mountain list: Mount Meru (Hindu), Yggdrasil/World Tree (Norse), Mount Olympus (Greek), Mount Qaf (Islamic), Harā Bərəz/Mount Hara (Zoroastrian), Mount Kunlun (Chinese) — all standard scholarly attributions, no fabrication
monitor.py 39 domain dictionaries at commit 8e4bf9b; 28 currently active — consistent with sections.json part2b
69 confirmed / 94.5% scorecard figures — consistent with dome site homepage usage (per c3/c4 prior verification)
citations_failed: Minor: '1 Enoch 17-18 then fills in a visionary cosmic geography with seven sacred mountains at the earth's center' — 1 Enoch 18:6-9 locates the seven magnificent mountains 'to the west' beyond the waters, not at the earth's center. The central throne-mountain passage is more clearly in 1 Enoch 24-25. See holes_found #3.
citations_unchecked: Clifford 'The Cosmic Mountain in Canaan and the Old Testament' and Levenson 'Sinai and Zion' — cited in EXP-216 integration_notes but not in wins.json prose; standard references, not attackable
Whether the dome site's current text still exactly matches the EXP-216 fetched_at quote — fresh fetch would confirm, but not required for this review

advocate_mode.best_defense: A patient dome defender could argue: 'You have conceded in paragraph 2 that the six-text convergence is genuine and the Zaphon-Zion typology is scholarly. You have then tried to dismiss it on three grounds — (a) retrodiction, (b) non-discriminating archetype, (c) scorecard accounting — but each is a methodological pre-commitment rather than a falsification. (a) would define any scriptural support for any cosmology out of the evidence space. (b) would define any archetype-based claim out of the evidence space (are you going to apply this equally when a spherical-earth cosmology cites the same mountains as supporting evidence?). (c) depends on the author's own monitor.py being the sole arbiter of what counts, which is itself a rhetorical choice. Meanwhile, you have conceded the lexical-geographic pattern IS real — ancient Israelites really did treat north as sacred because a real mountain was really in that direction — which is a flat-earth cartographic fact, which is half the dome's thesis. The critique, even at its most polished, reduces to "your evidence is real but we have excluded it by definition."'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Two small edits close most of the residual defense. (1) Add a sentence to argument (2) clarifying that 'north as cartographic direction' is equally compatible with a sphere (toward the pole) or dome disc — the Zaphon/ṣāpôn pattern reflects shared ANE cartography, not disc-specific geometry (holes_found #2). (2) Extend detail_verdict_text with a scorecard-accounting clause so the preemptive travels at the Verdict-box surface as well as Evidence/TLDR/finding (holes_found #1). These are the two moves that drop defense_survives from 2 to ≤1.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Unfalsifiable category heterogeneity across WIN-031 (untestable by construction), WIN-032 (untestable by construction), and WIN-034 (has an empirical refutation but verdict stays Unfalsifiable) — pre-existing issue, not introduced by EXP-216, and not WIN-031's to fix. Raise on WIN-034 next review. No new inconsistencies from this patch.

quantitative_verification.computations: All counts consistent with diff. No mathematical claim in the WIN itself; the scorecard figure (94.5% of 69 confirmed) is dome-site-sourced not independently computed.
